summary: Alfred is a highly comprehensible codebase with strong documentation (6 ADRs), clean architecture (5-crate workspace with zero circular dependencies), and thorough testing (209 tests). The primary comprehensibility risks are the large app.rs hotspot (2721 lines) and the bridge.rs complexity (1675 lines with 15+ registered primitives).

documentation_coverage.notes: Every module has //! doc comments. Every public function has /// doc comments. CLAUDE.md serves as project README. No standalone README.md file.

architecture_clarity.naming_notes: Consistent module-level free function style (e.g., cursor::move_down, buffer::insert_at, viewport::adjust). EditorState uses struct with methods only for new(). All domain operations are free functions taking &state or &mut state.

crates.name: alfred-core
crates.purpose: Pure domain layer: buffer, cursor, viewport, commands, hooks, keymaps, key events, error types

crates.name: alfred-lisp
crates.purpose: Lisp interpreter integration: LispRuntime wrapper, bridge primitives connecting Lisp to EditorState

crates.name: alfred-plugin
crates.purpose: Plugin system: discovery (scan plugins/ dir), metadata parsing, registry, lifecycle, topological sort

crates.name: alfred-tui
crates.purpose: Imperative shell: event loop, terminal rendering, key conversion, gutter/status computation

crates.name: alfred-bin
crates.purpose: Binary entry point: CLI parsing, composition root, plugin loading

plugins.name: vim-keybindings
plugins.description: Vim-style modal editing (hjkl, modes, x, d, i)

plugins.name: line-numbers
plugins.description: Gutter line numbers via render-gutter hook

top_hotspots.risk: Largest file, most changed. Contains event loop, key dispatch, gutter/status computation, and 61 tests. Candidate for decomposition.
Grows linearly with each new primitive. 15 primitives registered. Consider grouping by domain.
Aggregate root with builtin commands. Moderate but stable.
Test-heavy (acceptance + unit tests for full plugin lifecycle). Mostly test code.
Moderate size but low change frequency. Stable rendering layer.

test_quality.test_style: Acceptance tests first (Given/When/Then), unit tests for behaviors. Test budget: behaviors x 2.

recommendations.title: Decompose app.rs
recommendations.description: Split the 2721-line app.rs into focused modules: event_loop.rs (run function), key_dispatch.rs (handle_key_event, DeferredAction), gutter.rs (compute_gutter_content), status.rs (compute_status_content). Tests can remain co-located or move to a tests/ subdirectory.

recommendations.title: Group bridge primitives by domain
recommendations.description: Reorganize bridge.rs (1675 lines) into sub-modules: bridge/buffer.rs, bridge/cursor.rs, bridge/keymap.rs, bridge/hook.rs. Each module registers its own primitives.

recommendations.title: Add a README.md
recommendations.description: The project has CLAUDE.md but no traditional README.md. Add a README with project description, build instructions, architecture overview, and plugin development guide.

recommendations.title: Store PluginRegistry on EditorState
recommendations.description: Currently the PluginRegistry is consumed in main.rs and not stored. For runtime plugin reload/unload, it needs to be persisted on EditorState.

recommendations.title: Document the DeferredAction pattern
recommendations.description: The DeferredAction pattern (and the ClonedHandler pattern) solved real RefCell double-borrow bugs. Document these as an ADR or architecture note for future contributors.

score_breakdown.documentation_notes: 6 ADRs (excellent), strong inline docs, but no README.md. API docs present via module-level and function-level comments.
score_breakdown.architecture_notes: Clean 4-layer architecture, zero circular dependencies, compile-time enforcement via Cargo crates, consistent naming, single clear entry point.
score_breakdown.hotspot_notes: One significant hotspot (app.rs at 2721 lines, 22 changes). Bridge.rs is secondary. Rest of codebase is well-decomposed.
